title: <div>  •  No path feels heavier than another—just different learning styles.</di
content:
• No path feels heavier than another—just different learning styles.
• Nothing here is busy work. Everything has a purpose in helping students develop real-world skills.

4️⃣ Are You Designing This Course the Most Efficient Way?

You’re iterating correctly, but you’re getting caught in revision loops because you’re editing micro & macro elements simultaneously.

Suggested Workflow Refinement:
1. Set Your “10 Commandments” (Fixed Protocols) First
• These do not change. They act as the immovable framework.
• Ex: Weekly structure, student paths, grading flexibility.
2. Lock in the Macro Schedule Before Fine-Tuning Daily Plans
• Stop adjusting the master schedule after Week 3 unless absolutely necessary.
• Once a week is finalized, it should only be edited for clarity—no structural changes.
3. Create “Plug & Play” Templates for Daily Schedules
• Instead of customizing every day from scratch, use pre-built daily templates.
• Ex: A Monday always has the same structure, with only the content changing.
context: From document: Comprehensive Course Structure and Alternating Schedule (A & B Weeks… — chunk 75 (chunk 75/108)
